I need to modify the `CommentWrappingRule` to check if the preceding element is `{` (LBRACE) and the following element is `}` (RBRACE), and if so, allow this case as a single-line block with a block comment.

 21→/**
 22→ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered. A block comment following another element on the
 23→ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
 24→ */
 25→public class CommentWrappingRule :
 26→    StandardRule(
 27→        id = "comment-wrapping",
 28→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 29→            setOf(
 30→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 31→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 32→            ),
 33→    ) {
 34→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 35→        node: ASTNode,
 36→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 37→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 38→    ) {
 39→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 40→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 41→                node
 42→                    .prevLeaf()
 43→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 44→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 45→                node
 46→                    .nextLeaf()
 47→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 48→
 49→            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 50→                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 51→            ) {
 52→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 53→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 54→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 55→                    //    some comment
 56→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 57→                    emit(
 58→                        node.startOffset,
 59→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 60→                            "disallowed",
 61→                        false,
 62→                    )
 63→                } else if (isBlockCommentInSingleLineBlock(
 64→                        nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment,
 65→                        nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment,
 66→                    )
 67→                ) {
 68→                    // Allow single line blocks containing a block comment
 69→                    //    val foo = { /* no-op */ }
 70→                    return
 71→                } else {
 72→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 73→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 74→                    emit(
 75→                        node.startOffset,
 76→                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 77→                        false,
 78→                    )
 79→                }
 80→                return
 81→            }
 82→
 83→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 84→                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 85→
 86→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 87→                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 88→        }
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 92→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 93→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 95→    ) {
 96→        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 97→        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 98→            emit(
 99→                startOffset,
100→                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
101→                true,
102→            )
103→            if (autoCorrect) {
104→                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
105→            }
106→        } else {
107→            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
108→            emit(
109→                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
110→                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
111→                false,
112→            )
113→        }
114→    }
115→
116→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
117→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
118→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
119→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
120→        rawRemove()
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
124→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
125→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→    ) {
128→        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)
129→        if (autoCorrect) {
130→            this.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(blockCommentNode.indent())
131→        }
132→    }
133→
134→    private fun isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it: ASTNode) = it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !it.textContains('\n')
135→
136→    private fun ASTNode?.isLastElementOnLine() = this == null || (elementType == WHITE_SPACE && textContains('\n'))
137→
138→    private fun isBlockCommentInSingleLineBlock(
139→        precedingLeaf: ASTNode?,
140→        followingLeaf: ASTNode?,
141→    ): Boolean {
142→        val actualPreceding =
143→            if (precedingLeaf?.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
144→                precedingLeaf.prevLeaf()
145→            } else {
146→                precedingLeaf
147→            }
148→        val actualFollowing =
149→            if (followingLeaf?.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
150→                followingLeaf.nextLeaf()
151→            } else {
152→                followingLeaf
153→            }
154→        return actualPreceding?.elementType == LBRACE && actualFollowing?.elementType == RBRACE
155→    }
156→}

content.thinking:
One test failed. The test `Given a single line lambda with block comment as parameter then do not report error` failed. Let me investigate why. The issue is:

```kotlin
val list = listOf(1, 2, 3).filter { /* always true */ true }
```

In this case, the block comment `/* always true */` is followed by `true`, not by `}`. So it's not inside a "pure" single-line block comment scenario.

The fix I implemented only allows the case where the block comment is directly between `{` and `}`. But in this case, there's code after the comment (`true`).

Actually, looking at the original issue more carefully, it says:
> Give an singe line block containing a block comment than do not wrap this comment to a multiline block. So code below should be kept unchanged:
> ```
> val foo = { /* no-op */ }
> ```

This implies the block comment is the ONLY content of the block. So the test I wrote is incorrect - the block with `{ /* always true */ true }` is a different case.

Let me remove that test since it's not part of the original issue.

 21→/**

 23→ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.

 25→public class CommentWrappingRule :
 26→    StandardRule(

 31→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 32→            ),
 33→    ) {
 34→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 35→        node: ASTNode,
 36→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 37→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 38→    ) {
 39→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 40→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 41→                node
 42→                    .prevLeaf()
 43→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 44→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 45→                node
 46→                    .nextLeaf()
 47→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 48→
 49→            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 50→                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 51→            ) {
 52→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 53→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 54→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 55→                    //    some comment
 56→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 57→                    emit(
 58→                        node.startOffset,
 59→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 60→                            "disallowed",
 61→                        false,
 62→                    )
 63→                } else if (isBlockCommentInSingleLineBlock(
 64→                        nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment,
 65→                        nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment,
 66→                    )
 67→                ) {
 68→                    // Allow single line blocks containing a block comment
 69→                    //    val foo = { /* no-op */ }
 70→                    return
 71→                } else {
 72→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 73→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 74→                    emit(
 75→                        node.startOffset,
 76→                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 77→                        false,
 78→                    )
 79→                }
 80→                return
 81→            }
 82→
 83→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 84→                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 85→
 86→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 87→                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 88→        }
 89→    }

 93→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 95→    ) {
 96→        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 97→        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 98→            emit(
 99→                startOffset,
100→                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
101→                true,
102→            )
103→            if (autoCorrect) {
104→                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
105→            }
106→        } else {
107→            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
108→            emit(
109→                blockCommentNode.startOffset,

111→                false,
112→            )
113→        }
114→    }

117→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
118→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
119→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
120→        rawRemove()
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
124→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
125→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→    ) {
128→        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)
129→        if (autoCorrect) {
130→            this.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(blockCommentNode.indent())
131→        }
132→    }

134→    private fun isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it: ASTNode) = it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !it.textContains('\n')
135→
136→    private fun ASTNode?.isLastElementOnLine() = this == null || (elementType == WHITE_SPACE && textContains('\n'))
137→
138→    private fun isBlockCommentInSingleLineBlock(
139→        precedingLeaf: ASTNode?,
140→        followingLeaf: ASTNode?,
141→    ): Boolean {
142→        val actualPreceding =
143→            if (precedingLeaf?.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
144→                precedingLeaf.prevLeaf()
145→            } else {
146→                precedingLeaf
147→            }
148→        val actualFollowing =
149→            if (followingLeaf?.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
150→                followingLeaf.nextLeaf()
151→            } else {
152→                followingLeaf
153→            }
154→        return actualPreceding?.elementType == LBRACE && actualFollowing?.elementType == RBRACE
155→    }

The implementation is complete. Let me provide a summary of the changes made:

## Summary of Changes

### Problem
The `CommentWrappingRule` was incorrectly reporting an error for block comments inside single-line blocks like `val foo = { /* no-op */ }`, flagging it as "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed".

### Solution
Modified `/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/CommentWrappingRule.kt`:

1. **Added imports** for `LBRACE` and `RBRACE` element types to detect curly braces.

2. **Added a new condition** in the `beforeVisitChildNodes` method to check if the block comment is inside a single-line block (between `{` and `}`). If so, the rule returns early without emitting an error.

3. **Added a helper function** `isBlockCommentInSingleLineBlock()` that:
   - Looks at the actual non-whitespace elements surrounding the block comment
   - Returns `true` if the preceding element is `LBRACE` (`{`) and the following element is `RBRACE` (`}`)

### Test Coverage
Created a new test file `/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/CommentWrappingRuleSingleLineBlockTest.kt` with tests for:
- Single line block containing only a block comment with spaces: `val foo = { /* no-op */ }`
- Single line block containing only a block comment without spaces: `val foo = {/* no-op */}`
- Single line lambda in function call: `bar { /* no-op */ }`
- Verifying that block comments between non-brace elements still report errors: `val foo /* comment */ = "foo"`

 36→public class SpacingAroundCurlyRule : StandardRule("curly-spacing") {
 37→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 38→        node: ASTNode,
 39→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 40→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 41→    ) {
 42→        if (node.isLeaf() && !node.isPartOfString()) {
 43→            val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
 44→            val nextLeaf = node.nextLeaf()
 45→            val spacingBefore: Boolean
 46→            val spacingAfter: Boolean
 47→            if (node.elementType == LBRACE) {
 48→                spacingBefore =
 49→                    prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace ||
 50→                    prevLeaf?.elementType == AT ||
 51→                    (
 52→                        prevLeaf?.elementType == LPAR &&
 53→                            ((node as LeafPsiElement).parent is KtLambdaExpression || node.parent.parent is KtLambdaExpression)
 54→                        )
 55→                spacingAfter = nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace || nextLeaf?.elementType == RBRACE
 56→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace &&
 57→                    !prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
 58→                    prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.let {
 59→                        it.elementType == LPAR || it.elementType == AT
 60→                    } == true
 61→                ) {
 62→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected space before \"${node.text}\"", true)
 63→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 64→                        prevLeaf.node.treeParent.removeChild(prevLeaf.node)
 65→                    }
 66→                }
 67→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
 68→                    (
 69→                        prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.let { it.elementType == RPAR || KtTokens.KEYWORDS.contains(it.elementType) } == true ||
 70→                            node.treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 71→                            (prevLeaf.treeParent.elementType == FUN && prevLeaf.treeNext.elementType != LAMBDA_EXPRESSION)
 72→                        ) // allow newline for lambda return type
 73→                ) {
 74→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected newline before \"${node.text}\"", true)
 75→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 76→                        val eolCommentExists =
 77→                            prevLeaf
 78→                                .prevLeaf()
 79→                                ?.let { it is PsiComment && it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
 80→                                ?: false
 81→                        if (eolCommentExists) {
 82→                            val commentLeaf = prevLeaf.prevLeaf()!!
 83→                            if (commentLeaf.prevLeaf() is PsiWhiteSpace) {
 84→                                (commentLeaf.prevLeaf() as LeafPsiElement).rawRemove()
 85→                            }
 86→                            (node.treeParent.treeParent as TreeElement).removeChild(commentLeaf)
 87→                            (node.treeParent as TreeElement).addChild(commentLeaf, node.treeNext)
 88→                            node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
 89→                        }
 90→                        (prevLeaf as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
 91→                    }
 92→                }
 93→            } else if (node.elementType == RBRACE) {
 94→                spacingBefore = prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace || prevLeaf?.elementType == LBRACE
 95→                spacingAfter = nextLeaf == null || nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace || shouldNotToBeSeparatedBySpace(nextLeaf)
 96→                if (nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && !nextLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
 97→                    shouldNotToBeSeparatedBySpace(nextLeaf.nextLeaf())
 98→                ) {
 99→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected space after \"${node.text}\"", true)
100→                    if (autoCorrect) {
101→                        nextLeaf.node.treeParent.removeChild(nextLeaf.node)
102→                    }
103→                }
104→            } else {
105→                return
106→            }
107→            when {
108→                !spacingBefore && !spacingAfter -> {
109→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Missing spacing around \"${node.text}\"", true)
110→                    if (autoCorrect) {
111→                        node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
112→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
113→                    }
114→                }
115→                !spacingBefore -> {
116→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Missing spacing before \"${node.text}\"", true)
117→                    if (autoCorrect) {
118→                        node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
119→                    }
120→                }
121→                !spacingAfter -> {
122→                    emit(node.startOffset + 1, "Missing spacing after \"${node.text}\"", true)
123→                    if (autoCorrect) {
124→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
125→                    }
126→                }
127→            }
128→        }
129→    }
130→
131→    private fun shouldNotToBeSeparatedBySpace(leaf: ASTNode?): Boolean {
132→        val nextElementType = leaf?.elementType
133→        return (
134→            nextElementType == DOT ||
135→                nextElementType == COMMA ||
136→                nextElementType == RBRACKET ||
137→                nextElementType == RPAR ||
138→                nextElementType == SEMICOLON ||
139→                nextElementType == SAFE_ACCESS ||
140→                nextElementType == EXCLEXCL ||
141→                nextElementType == LBRACKET ||
142→                nextElementType == LPAR ||
143→                nextElementType == COLONCOLON
144→            )
145→    }
146→}

 21→/**
 22→ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered. A block comment following another element on the
 23→ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
 24→ */
 25→public class CommentWrappingRule :
 26→    StandardRule(
 27→        id = "comment-wrapping",
 28→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 29→            setOf(
 30→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 31→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 32→            ),
 33→    ) {
 34→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 35→        node: ASTNode,
 36→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 37→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 38→    ) {
 39→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 40→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 41→                node
 42→                    .prevLeaf()
 43→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 44→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 45→                node
 46→                    .nextLeaf()
 47→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 48→
 49→            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 50→                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 51→            ) {
 52→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 53→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 54→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 55→                    //    some comment
 56→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 57→                    emit(
 58→                        node.startOffset,
 59→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 60→                            "disallowed",
 61→                        false,
 62→                    )
 63→                } else if (isBlockCommentInSingleLineBlock(
 64→                        nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment,
 65→                        nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment,
 66→                    )
 67→                ) {
 68→                    // Allow single line blocks containing a block comment
 69→                    //    val foo = { /* no-op */ }
 70→                    return
 71→                } else {
 72→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 73→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 74→                    emit(
 75→                        node.startOffset,
 76→                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 77→                        false,
 78→                    )
 79→                }
 80→                return
 81→            }
 82→
 83→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 84→                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 85→
 86→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 87→                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 88→        }
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 92→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 93→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 95→    ) {
 96→        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 97→        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 98→            emit(
 99→                startOffset,
100→                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
101→                true,
102→            )
103→            if (autoCorrect) {
104→                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
105→            }
106→        } else {
107→            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
108→            emit(
109→                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
110→                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
111→                false,
112→            )
113→        }
114→    }
115→
116→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
117→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
118→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
119→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
120→        rawRemove()
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
124→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
125→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→    ) {
128→        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)
129→        if (autoCorrect) {
130→            this.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(blockCommentNode.indent())
131→        }
132→    }
133→
134→    private fun isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it: ASTNode) = it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !it.textContains('\n')
135→
136→    private fun ASTNode?.isLastElementOnLine() = this == null || (elementType == WHITE_SPACE && textContains('\n'))
137→
138→    private fun isBlockCommentInSingleLineBlock(
139→        precedingLeaf: ASTNode?,
140→        followingLeaf: ASTNode?,
141→    ): Boolean {
142→        val actualPreceding =
143→            if (precedingLeaf?.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
144→                precedingLeaf.prevLeaf()
145→            } else {
146→                precedingLeaf
147→            }
148→        val actualFollowing =
149→            if (followingLeaf?.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
150→                followingLeaf.nextLeaf()
151→            } else {
152→                followingLeaf
153→            }
154→        return actualPreceding?.elementType == LBRACE && actualFollowing?.elementType == RBRACE
155→    }
156→}

 33→    ) {
 34→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 35→        node: ASTNode,
 36→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 37→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 38→    ) {
 39→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 40→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 41→                node
 42→                    .prevLeaf()
 43→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 44→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 45→                node
 46→                    .nextLeaf()
 47→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 48→
 49→            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 50→                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 51→            ) {
 52→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 53→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 54→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 55→                    //    some comment
 56→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 57→                    emit(
 58→                        node.startOffset,
 59→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 60→                            "disallowed",
 61→                        false,
 62→                    )
 63→                } else if (isBlockCommentInSingleLineBlock(
 64→                        nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment,
 65→                        nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment,
 66→                    )
 67→                ) {
 68→                    // Allow single line blocks containing a block comment
 69→                    //    val foo = { /* no-op */ }
 70→                    return
 71→                } else {
 72→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 73→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 74→                    emit(
 75→                        node.startOffset,
 76→                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 77→                        false,
 78→                    )
 79→                }
 80→                return
 81→            }
 82→
 83→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 84→                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 85→
 86→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 87→                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 88→        }
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 92→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 93→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 95→    ) {
 96→        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 97→        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 98→            emit(
 99→                startOffset,
100→                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
101→                true,
102→            )
103→            if (autoCorrect) {
104→                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
105→            }
106→        } else {
107→            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
108→            emit(
109→                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
110→                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
111→                false,
112→            )
113→        }
114→    }
115→
116→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
117→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
118→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
119→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
120→        rawRemove()
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
124→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
125→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→    ) {
128→        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)
129→        if (autoCorrect) {
130→            this.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(blockCommentNode.indent())
131→        }
132→    }
133→
134→    private fun isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it: ASTNode) = it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !it.textContains('\n')
135→
136→    private fun ASTNode?.isLastElementOnLine() = this == null || (elementType == WHITE_SPACE && textContains('\n'))
137→
138→    private fun isBlockCommentInSingleLineBlock(
139→        precedingLeaf: ASTNode?,
140→        followingLeaf: ASTNode?,
141→    ): Boolean {
142→        val actualPreceding =
143→            if (precedingLeaf?.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
144→                precedingLeaf.prevLeaf()
145→            } else {
146→                precedingLeaf
147→            }
148→        val actualFollowing =
149→            if (followingLeaf?.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
150→                followingLeaf.nextLeaf()
151→            } else {
152→                followingLeaf
153→            }
154→        return actualPreceding?.elementType == LBRACE && actualFollowing?.elementType == RBRACE
155→    }
156→}
157→
158→public val COMMENT_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = CommentWrappingRule().ruleId
